items.title: GPS hotspot detection for urban events
items.scenario: A city analytics team collects anonymized phone GPS pings during a festival. Dense pockets near stages and food courts should become clusters; scattered pings in transit corridors are noise.
items.explanation: DBSCAN on (lat, lon) after scaling finds irregular hotspots without fixing k. eps captures walkable venue radius; min_samples filters one-off pings. Noise label −1 marks commuters, not event clusters.

items.title: Network intrusion grouping by flow features
items.scenario: Security engineers cluster connection logs by bytes/sec, packet count, and duration. Most traffic forms a few dense normal modes; rare sparse flows may be scans or misconfiguration.
items.explanation: Density-based clustering separates bulk normal traffic from sparse anomalies. Points labeled noise flag candidates for analyst review — unlike k-means, they are not forced into a cluster.

items.title: Retail store catchment from customer addresses
items.scenario: A chain geocodes loyalty members per store region. Marketing wants natural geographic blobs (suburbs, corridors) rather than k equal-sized territories.
items.explanation: DBSCAN on projected coordinates finds elongated commuter belts and compact neighborhoods. Varying store density is handled better than k-means with fixed k, though very sparse rural areas may need eps tuning.

items.title: Astronomy: grouping star positions in a patch
items.scenario: An observatory pipeline clusters stellar coordinates in a small sky patch to find open clusters and galaxy overdensities amid background field stars.
items.explanation: Arbitrary-shaped overdensities suit density clustering. min_samples suppresses single-pixel artifacts; noise points are field stars not belonging to a physical group.

items.title: IoT sensor fault modes in embedding space
items.scenario: Factory sensors emit 20-dimensional embeddings from an autoencoder. Engineers cluster healthy operating modes and treat low-density regions as novel fault signatures.
items.explanation: DBSCAN discovers mode count automatically when faults are rare (noise). Feature scaling is mandatory before eps selection; evaluation uses Cluster Evaluation on non-noise assignments.
